notes: This file is repo-side only. AIP requires operating the intake_endpoint on infrastructure you control.
If you later host schema/intake on a custom domain, update the URLs here (or leave AIP unused and follow llms.txt fallback entrypoints).
For deterministic intake health/fallback behavior, see docs/agent_intake_status.md and docs/agent_intake_roundtrip.sample.json.

degraded_when: invalid JSON or wrong schema version

privacy.purpose: Return curated entrypoints (files/scripts) and optional context pack URLs; optionally log coarse task tags for aggregate usage.
do_not_send: raw prompts (unless the user explicitly chooses to share them)

fallback_entrypoints.why: Top-level product, setup, and release-status overview.
Repo workflow, worktree rules, and verified build commands.
Fastest current task routing for agent/runtime work.
Current shipped Agent Run, observable-driver, and runtime constraints.
Machine-readable summary of current actions, affordances, and route profiles.
Deterministic intake health/fallback cue for intake-first routing.
Compact request/response/next-action intake example.
Docs index for the public repo surface and current feature docs.
Prepared-runtime contract for local Magic Select and warm-click flows.
Allowlist for remaining brood/juggernaut internal identifiers.
Current public env surface with CUE-first naming and BROOD compatibility aliases.
Planner prompt contract, context summary, action parsing, and export/control actions.
Goal-contract compile and stop-check schemas, prompts, and parsers.
Lower-level visible-canvas automation contract for replay and observable actions.
Authoritative seeded-tool and direct-affordance routing with current route profile ids.
Create Tool manifest builder, tool schemas, and custom-tool runtime.
Shell rail ordering, visibility, and surfaced affordances.
Primary native CLI entry and realtime/env plumbing for the packaged engine.
Native engine orchestration and provider/env behavior.
Tauri backend glue, filesystem scope, and desktop env handling.
FS scope and app configuration (common cause of import/export issues).
Dev runner that starts the Tauri app with native Rust runtime defaults.

agent-runtime.description: Agent Run planner behavior, action parsing, and planner-visible constraints.

agent-goal-contract.description: Goal compile, hard-requirement extraction, and stop checks for Agent Run.

agent-observable.description: Visible-canvas automation, replay, and Magic Select prepared-runtime behavior.

single-image-routing.description: Seeded tools, direct affordances, and current route profile ids.

tool-runtime.description: Create Tool manifest generation, tool schemas, and custom-tool invocation.

desktop-dev.description: Run the desktop app locally; fix build/startup issues.

desktop-quick-actions.description: Change seeded-tool or direct-affordance UI/behavior in the current shell.

desktop-canvas.description: Canvas interactions, selections, tools, layout, filmstrip behavior.

desktop-tauri.description: Tauri backend, FS scope, command wiring, native build issues.

engine-rust.description: Native Rust engine commands, orchestration, contracts, and compatibility safeguards.

engine-cli.description: Cue CLI commands and flags, native Rust runtime.

engine-providers.description: Provider integrations, model routing, and API key handling in the native runtime.

engine-recreate.description: Recreate flow (reference image in, similarities/edits out).

engine-memory.description: Opt-in memory system, storage, and retrieval logic.

engine-events.description: Run artifacts + events.jsonl format; stability of event stream consumed by desktop.

tests.description: Rust + desktop test coverage and validation commands.

docs.description: Docs updates for the public repo surface and agent/runtime references.

task.notes: Need to adjust Agent Run or a single-image affordance without drifting from the current public runtime contracts.
